items.title: Thai farmers pin hopes on microbes to end annual burning crisis
items.summary: RICE farmers Siriporn and Amnat Taidee used to burn their paddy fields between plantings – a common method of clearing crop residue partly blamed for toxic smog that blankets much of Thailand every spring. "My life has changed," said Siriporn, 63, as she sloshed through a verdant paddy field in Chiang Rai. [[nid:1419257]] Last year, authorities in Chiang Rai – one of Thailand's main rice-growing provinces – began encouraging local farmers to try microbial solutions.

items.title: Police cripple violent Sarawak gang, 21 arrested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have crippled a violent gang operating in Sarawak with the arrest of 21 local men in a coordinated crackdown under Op Pusak Merah. The operation, launched on April 15, was carried out jointly by Bukit Aman's Criminal Investigation Department (CID) D9 unit and the Sarawak CID, targeting suspects linked to a string of violent crimes since 2024. CID director Datuk M. Kumar said the suspects, aged between 19 and 44, were detained in separate raids across Kota Samarahan, Kuching, Simunjan and Padawan.

items.title: Johor Immigration denies closing counters for Raya potluck
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Immigration Department has denied viral social media claims that its counters at Setia Tropika here were closed for an internal Hari Raya potluck party. Its director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said the counters operated as usual and no closures were implemented for the festivities. "There were no instructions to close the counters; it was only an internal potluck Raya celebration.

items.title: Armed robbers hit Brickfields gold shop, flee with RM9.7m jewellery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — Four armed men reportedly robbed a gold shop on Jalan Tun Sambanthan here this morning, with losses estimated at up to RM9.7 million. Kuala Lumpur Police Chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the suspects entered the premises at about 11am before brandishing two pistols and fleeing with jewellery, according to report in Sinar Harian . A shotgun and a bullet belonging to the shop’s security guard were also reported missing.

items.title: Uzma order book to hit up to RM4.5bil on new contracts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Uzma Bhd's order book is estimated to rise to between RM4.3 billion and RM4.5 billion after securing three coiled tubing unit (CTU) contracts from Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd, providing earnings visibility of up to five years. Public Investment Bank Bhd (PublicInvest) said that while the contract value was not disclosed due to its call-out nature, it estimates a potential value of RM500 million to RM700 million over the contract period. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Muhyiddin trial: Bankers detail RM5mil transactions to Bersatu
items.summary: Alfian: Agreed. Alfian: Agreed. Alfian: Agreed.

items.title: UiTM vice-chancellor receives national education leadership award
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) vice-chancellor, Prof Datuk Dr Shahrin Sahib, has received the Malaysian Outstanding Education Icon Leadership Excellence Award. The award recognised Shahrin's role in strengthening institutional direction, advancing academic excellence and supporting broader education development through inclusive and innovation-driven leadership. Shahrin said leadership in higher education goes beyond administrative responsibilities.

items.title: Perdana Petroleum secures Petronas Carigali work orders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Perdana Petroleum Bhd has secured two work orders from Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd for the provision of anchor handling tug and supply vessels. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the group said the contracts were awarded to its wholly-owned subsidiary, Perdana Nautika Sdn Bhd. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Malaysia on track for AI nation status by 2030 despite Middle East conflict
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: The Digital Ministry remains confident that Malaysia is on track to achieve its vision of becoming an artificial intelligence (AI) nation by 2030, despite the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the country must nonetheless prepare for any future impacts that could affect the vision. Gobind said the target for the digital economy to contribute 30 per cent to the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) by 2030 remains unchanged.

items.title: Crisis offers chance for businesses to reposition, says Steven Sim
items.summary: Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong said that despite concerns over subsidy limits and rising costs, substantial government support remains in place to help businesses navigate the environment. Indeed, the world, not only Malaysia, is facing significant challenges," he said after Bank Kerjasama Rakyat Malaysia Bhd's financial performance and dividend announcement today. On fuel, he said the scale of subsidies is often underestimated.

items.title: DBKL, CIMB partner to boost KL's community, economic growth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Bandaraya Kuala Lumpur (DBKL) and CIMB Group have announced a collaboration aimed at strengthening economic growth and community development in the capital. Dubbed "Moving KL Forward – Kita Bagi Jadi", the initiative was formalised through a three-year memorandum of collaboration signed today. CIMB Islamic Bhd chief executive officer Ahmad Shahriman Mohd Shariff said CIMB has allocated RM5 million over three years for the initiative, which will be implemented in collaboration with DBKL through carefully planned programmes.

items.title: Firm used overdraft to pay Bersatu RM1m in 2022, court hears in Muhyiddin abuse trial
items.summary: Maybank Kota Kemuning branch’s assistant manager Alfian Othman, 57, confirmed Nepturis Sdn Bhd had taken overdrafts from Maybank in February 2022, including for a RM1 million sum paid to Bersatu. Earlier, Alfian verified that this RM1 million had indeed entered Bersatu’s AmBank account (account number 8881028867041), based on the latter’s bank statement. — Picture by Sayuti Zainudin Based on both Azman’s CIMB bank statements and Bersatu’s CIMB bank statements, Herdayanti verified that these four cheques from Azman had entered Bersatu’s account: RM2 million (cheque number 73659) paid on September 24, 2021; RM1 million (cheque number 73660) paid on September 24, 2021; RM500,000 (cheque number 73665) paid on November 3, 2022; RM500,000 (cheque number 73666) paid on November 3, 2022.

items.title: Felda eyes wider use of B100 biodiesel
items.summary: KUANTAN: The rollout of B100, a 100 per cent palm oil-based biodiesel, is set to expand beyond Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) vehicles to include plantation machinery. Felda chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek said discussions on extending the use of B100 to palm oil processing machinery at Felda schemes were held last week, with Kuantan likely to be the starting point as early as this month. Palm oil is used to make cooking oil, processed foods, cosmetics, detergents and biofuels.

items.title: Family wants inspector in attempted murder case of cop placed in hospital
items.summary: Their wish was conveyed by defence lawyer Nanda Goban after Muhammad Kamal Raj Shanmugam Abdullah, 40, was charged before Sessions Court judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid. Mazuliana ordered Kamal to be sent to Hospital Bahagia Ulu Kinta in Perak for a one-month psychiatric evaluation. Kamal, he said, is unmarried and was previously based at the Kedah police headquarters.

items.title: 299 fake energy crisis posts removed, Facebook and TikTok top list
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Social media platforms have removed 299 pieces of fake content related to the global energy crisis following requests from the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the figure represents a 66 per cent "take down" rate out of 450 identified items flagged between March 29 and 7am today. Also present were Deputy Communications Minister Datuk Teo Nie Ching, Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah and Malaysian National News Agency (Bernama) chief executive officer Datin Paduka Nur-ul Afida Kamaludin.

items.title: In tough times, Russians are turning to magic
items.summary: SELF-DESCRIBED witch Natalia Malinovskaya has a new client base: men fighting in eastern Ukraine, who, like a growing number of Russians, are drawn to the supernatural against a backdrop of conflict and economic uncertainty. In her darkened apartment in Moscow, Malinovskaya, who says she inherited her powers from her grandmother, offers services from love spells to protection from evil. She has also made frequent appearances on Russian television.

items.title: Experts back move to align diesel subsidy with Budi95 system
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Experts have lauded the government's move to review the mechanism for targeted diesel subsidies, aligning it with the system currently implemented under the Budi95 initiative for RON95 petrol. University Utara Malaysia (UUM) economist Dr Irwan Shah Zainal Abidin said extending the Budi95 mechanism to diesel was both necessary and practical, given its effectiveness in curbing subsidy leakages. Meanwhile, UUM security studies expert Dr Faisol Keling said implementing a MyKad-based diesel subsidy mechanism could help stabilise prices and curb illicit activities.

items.title: Move it or lose it: DBKL goes on a 4,500-vehicle towing spree
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) has carried out more than 4,500 towing and removal actions between 2022 and 2025, sparked by over 9,600 public complaints regarding abandoned and obstructive vehicles. Fadlun said that DBKL’s services encompass towing and removal from public areas, storage, and sustainable disposal processes. Abandoned vehicles are collected for voluntary disposal at Block D, PPR Pantai Ria, Kuala Lumpur on April 16, 2026.

items.title: Unlicensed welfare home operator faces fresh child abuse charges
items.summary: GOMBAK: A 31-year-old man, who was previously charged with sexual assault, was brought before the Selayang Sessions and Magistrate's Courts today to face five new charges of abusing residents and operating an unlicensed children's welfare home. S. Valan, 31, the operator of the home, pleaded not guilty to all charges read before Sessions Court Judge Norazlin Othman and magistrate Chai Guan Hock. In the Sessions Court, Valan was charged with abusing four children under his care—two boys aged 11 and 17, and two girls aged 8 and 16—resulting in physical injuries.

items.title: Azalina: Parliament role risks political influence in Public Prosecutor appointment
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said this was because Parliament comprised politicians. She said the government was weighing whether the proposed constitutional amendment would require such political involvement. Azalina said the government aimed to find a middle ground by assessing demands from non-governmental organisations (NGOs) and civil society organisations (CSOs), which want Parliament to play a larger role in the appointment process.

items.title: Govt to enforce under-16 social media ban by June
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government plans to restrict social media access for children under 16 by late June as part of a major push to strengthen online protection. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the move is currently subject to a final review, with the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) conducting a regulatory sandbox with social media platforms. Also present were Deputy Communications Minister Datuk Teo Nie Ching, Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah and Bernama chief executive officer Datin Paduka Nur-ul Afida Kamaludin.

items.title: DBKL steps up enforcement every two days to tackle street photo touts in KLCC tourist hotspot
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) conducts joint enforcement operations with the police every two days to curb the activities of street photographers, or photo touts, in the Kuala Lumpur City Centre (KLCC) area. Mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud said at least five photo touts were detained during each operation, with their equipment also seized. He said that, in addition to enforcement, DBKL had also installed closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras to monitor the activity, together with a multilingual announcement system issuing alerts every 10 minutes cautioning tourists against using the services of photo touts.

items.title: South Korea, China to hold high-level economic talks next week amid Middle East crisis
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea and China will hold senior-level economic talks early next week to discuss joint efforts to ensure a stable environment for trade and investment amid the Middle East crisis, the foreign ministry said Thursday, Yonhap News Agency reported. Second Vice Foreign Minister Kim Jina will join Chinese Vice Commerce Minister Yan Dong in Beijing on Monday for the 29th South Korea-China Joint Economic Commission, Park Il, the ministry spokesperson, said in a press briefing. "In particular, the vice ministers are expected to discuss ways to work together to overcome the crisis, including expanding bilateral trade and investment and fostering a stable and predictable business environment, amid a global polycrisis triggered by the Middle East conflict," Park said.

items.title: Welfare home owner pleads not guilty to abusing four minors, operating unregistered Batu Caves centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — An owner of a children’s welfare home pleaded not guilty in separate courts today to charges of abusing four minors and operating an unregistered care centre. S. Valan, 31, who had custody of the four victims, comprising two boys aged 11 and 17, and two girls aged eight and 16, was charged at the Selayang Sessions Court with four counts of cruelty for allegedly causing physical harm to the victims. Last April 9, Valan pleaded not guilty at the Ampang Sessions Court to four counts of physical sexual abuse on four boys aged between 13 and 16, allegedly committed between 2019 and March this year.

items.title: Anwar and Albanese deepen food supply cooperation as global supply chains face pressure
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 16 — The Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (JAKIM) and Australia’s Department of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries today signed a memorandum of understanding (MoU) marking a key step in enhancing market access and strengthening food security cooperation between the two countries. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the agreement reflects both nations’ commitment to ensuring a sustainable food supply amid growing global uncertainties. “We also agreed to deepen cooperation in agriculture, including sharing expertise in production and irrigation to ensure a sustainable food supply for both Malaysians and Australians,” he said during a joint press conference with Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese after witnessing the MoU signing.

items.title: Selangor unveils RM130.4mil economic package to cushion Middle East crisis impact
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Selangor government today unveiled the first phase of its economic package involving an RM130.42 million allocation to mitigate the economic impact of the ongoing Middle East crisis. "The allocation for the Jelajah Ehsan Rahmah programme through the Selangor Agricultural Development Corporation has been increased by RM5 million, bringing the total to RM10 million this year. To ensure sufficient food supplies, the state government will allocate RM50 million for the Gudang Makanan Selangor (GMS) initiative, with RM25 million from state funds and another RM25 million from the corporation's reserves.

items.title: [UPDATED] Armed gang flees with RM9.7mil in jewellery after Brickfields heist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Four armed men made off with jewellery worth RM9.7 million after robbing a jewellery shop in Brickfields today. City police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the incident occurred around 11am today at a premises along Jalan Tun Sambanthan, when four unidentified suspects entered the store armed with two pistols. "The suspects are believed to have taken 21 trays of gold jewellery, weighing an estimated 15kg, before fleeing the scene.

items.title: Kepong MP offers RM5,000 reward for recapture of Pakistani fugitive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A member of parliament has announced a RM5,000 reward for information leading to the recapture of a Pakistani detainee who escaped during a transfer at Sungai Buloh Prison. "How does a detainee under full custody simply run off during a transfer? "Enough delay.

items.title: Syria begins loading Iraqi oil shipments for re-export
items.summary: SYRIA: Syria began loading its first tanker carrying Iraqi oil on Wednesday at the Baniyas port refinery, according to state media and an AFP correspondent, after Iraq was largely unable to export during the Middle East war. "The loading of the first oil tanker is underway in Syria today, under the agreement reached with the Iraqi side to transport Iraqi oil to the Baniyas refinery and then to the oil terminal for shipment by sea," Syrian Petroleum Company deputy CEO Ahmed Qubbaji told reporters. The Iraqi oil ministry said in early April that it had begun exporting oil by truck through neighbouring Syria.

items.title: Anwar: Reopening of Strait of Hormuz vital to ending Middle East crisis
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East must end with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, backed by clear guarantees to halt hostilities, attacks and sanctions, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said Malaysia has leveraged its close ties with Tehran to convey the concerns of Gulf and Arab leaders to the Iranian leadership, while using its position to promote peace and a negotiated resolution. "That is why, while supporting freedom of navigation, we also want categorical guarantees from countries — including big powers — to end hostilities, attacks and even sanctions against other countries.

items.title: AI demand drives chipmaker TSMC's net profit to fresh record
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwanese chip maker TSMC said Thursday its net profit surged to a fresh record in the first quarter, fuelled by the global artificial intelligence race despite the war in the Middle East. Massive demand for AI hardware means business is booming for TSMC -- the biggest contract maker of microchips used in everything from Apple phones to Nvidia processors. TSMC said net profit for the first three months of the year jumped 58.3 per cent year-on-year to NT$572.5 billion (US$18 billion), trouncing analyst estimates of NT$540.20 billion.

items.title: Selangor revises 2026 budget to mitigate Middle East conflict economic impact
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Selangor will revise its 2026 state budget to mitigate the economic impact of the escalating conflict involving the United States, Israel, and Iran, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said today. The revision, Amirudin said, will focus on addressing the rising cost of living, providing aid to affected people, supporting industries, and accelerating the transition to renewable energy. Amirudin said the decision followed a Selangor Economic Action Council (MTES) special meeting, which evaluated immediate measures to safeguard the state's economy.

items.title: Malaysia targets June rollout of social media curbs for under-16s, Fahmi says
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 16 — The government plans to impose restrictions on social media account access for children under the age of 16 as part of efforts to strengthen online protection. “The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is currently conducting a regulatory sandbox with social media platforms to identify mechanisms currently used by service providers. Fahmi said MCMC had held in-depth discussions on the proposed restrictions with social media platforms in Singapore last Friday.

items.title: Police bust counterfeit liquor syndicate in Negri Sembilan, Selangor
items.summary: Federal police Wildlife Crime Control Bureau/Special Investigation Intelligence (WCB/PSK) commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said the operation was carried out following two separate raids in Senawang, Negri Sembilan, and Beranang, Selangor, yesterday. "Seizures in the first raid included 95 boxes of assorted brands of alcoholic beverages, 18 cartons of non-dutiable cigarettes and six boxes of subsidised cooking oil from the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry in Seremban," he said in a statement. "In the second raid, police detained two foreign nationals from Myanmar and India respectively, who were caught transferring boxes containing suspected counterfeit alcoholic beverages.

items.title: Fahmi says 299 fake posts on global energy crisis removed as Malaysia intensifies online crackdown
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 16 — A total of 299 pieces of fake content related to the global energy crisis have been taken down by social media platform providers so far, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. He said the number was out of 450 pieces of fake content identified by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) to be taken down from March 29 to 7am this morning. “The platform that spread the most false information was Facebook with 180 pieces of content involving various issues, while the second most was TikTok with 134 pieces of content,” he said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Drug syndicate busted for hiding meth in food tins bound for Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have crippled a drug syndicate that concealed methamphetamine in food tins before attempting to smuggle the drugs into Sabah via courier services, with investigations revealing the network was also targeting lucrative markets in the Philippines. Bukit Aman Narcotic Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said police seized 61kg of methamphetamine worth RM3.05 million. "This price disparity is what drives syndicates to move drugs across borders, as the profits are far more lucrative in external markets," he said, adding that the syndicate would send the drugs to east Malaysian locations using courier services.

items.title: Drivers' confrontation in PJ resolved, case classified as NFA
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: A viral confrontation between two drivers at a traffic light junction along Jalan PJU 8/1 here has been resolved amicably and classified as no further action (NFA). District police chief, Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat, said the incident occurred at noon yesterday. He said both parties had lodged police reports over the matter.

items.title: [UPDATED] Ops Langka: Police cripple syndicate, seize RM13.68mil worth of cannabis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have crippled a drug syndicate following a major nationwide crackdown under Ops Langka, arresting 191 individuals and seizing drugs worth RM13.68 million. Bukit Aman Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the operation, conducted between April 9 and 12, targeted high-risk areas in Kedah, Perlis, Langkawi and Kuala Kedah. Hussein said a total of 231 raids were carried out, resulting in arrests for various offences under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and other related laws.

items.title: War-driven energy shock pushes Selangor to fast-track diversified energy mix
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 16 — The Selangor state government will accelerate its transition toward a more diversified and resilient energy mix, including exploring nuclear power, as geopolitical instability and global conflicts continue to disrupt traditional fuel supplies and drive up costs. In a statement addressing the state’s long-term energy security, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said Selangor can no longer rely solely on conventional sources like gas or coal, as the state looks to insulate its operations and the public from the volatility of global diesel and energy prices. “This means that the energy mix in Selangor must be diversified.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia to prioritise domestic fuel, supply surplus to Australia
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 16 — Malaysia will prioritise meeting its domestic fuel requirements before supplying any excess to Australia, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. “The priority is, of course, on domestic requirements and economic resilience in the country,” he said at a joint press conference with visiting Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese, here today. Malaysia, he said, imports gas, including liquefied natural gas (LNG), from Australia, which has reaffirmed its commitment to honour existing supply agreements, while Malaysia, in turn, will ensure that Australia’s requirements are fulfilled once domestic needs are secured.

items.title: More time needed to gauge impact of work from home policy, says Public Service chief
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, April 16 — The implementation of the Work From Home (BDR) policy for federal civil servants, which came into effect yesterday, has proceeded smoothly without disrupting public service delivery, said Director-General of Public Service (KPPA) Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz. However, he said more time is needed to assess the impact of its implementation. Meanwhile, Wan Ahmad Dahlan said the programme provided a platform for leaders to engage with civil servants and share their experiences.

items.title: ‘Better than doing nothing’: Hannah Yeoh defends Bangun KL, says it uses no public funds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh today reportedly defended the “Bangun KL” campaign following criticism, saying it is a private sector initiative and does not involve government spending. The initiative, launched on April 9, aims to encourage commuters to enter Kuala Lumpur earlier to ease peak-hour congestion through behavioural incentives and cross-sector collaboration. Yeoh said traffic jams were not unique to Kuala Lumpur, noting that many cities worldwide face similar problems.

items.title: Perak logs 4,848 online scams with RM125m losses, students increasingly targeted
items.summary: IPOH, April 16 — A total of 4,848 online fraud cases involving losses amounting to RM124.99 million were recorded in Perak last year, with a worrying rise in cases among students, the State Assembly was told today. State Communications, Multimedia and NGO Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi said data from the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) showed that online scams affected individuals across all segments of society in 2025, regardless of age, occupation or background, including students, civil servants, retirees and private sector workers. Mohd Azlan said the most common types of scams include telecommunications fraud, such as fake calls and phishing, followed by non-existent investment schemes, e-commerce scams, love scams, as well as increasingly sophisticated fraud involving artificial intelligence technology.

items.title: Breaking up is hard to do — Malaysia still seeking ‘middle ground’ in AG-Prosecutor split
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — The parliamentary select committee reviewing the proposed separation of the Attorney-General (AG) and Public Prosecutor roles is working to find a “middle ground,” with the goal of tabling a constitutional amendment by June. According to Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said, the committee is currently distilling feedback from civil society organisations (CSOs), non-governmental organisations (NGOs), and lawmakers. Their input has been very consistent with their long-held positions on this matter,” Azalina told reporters following the committee’s third meeting at Parliament today.

items.title: Tired of reckless drivers? Study shows 71pc of Malaysians support tougher traffic penalties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — Growing alarm over road safety in Malaysia has triggered an unusually strong public mandate for legislative and educational reform, as citizens grow increasingly weary of reckless driving and fatal accidents. According to the Ipsos Mobility Monitor 2026, 61 per cent of Malaysians expressed deep concern regarding road safety in their local areas. Approximately 71 per cent of Malaysians now actively support stricter traffic laws, surpassing the global average of 66 per cent.

items.title: Selangor sets aside RM134m to shield poor, small businesses from global energy crisis
items.summary: For food security, the package will add RM5 million to the Jelajah Ehsan Rahmah (JER) programme, which will be held once a month in every state constituency across Selangor. To increase food supply stocks, the state will allocate RM50 million for the Selangor Food Warehouse (GMS) — consisting of RM25 million from state funds and RM25 million from PKPS reserves. Amirudin said the Baucar Kita Selangor (Kita Selangor Voucher) worth RM100 per month will last for six months, benefiting 50,000 Selangor citizens.

items.title: Could a new pancreatic cancer drug double survival rates? Malaysian doctor leads breakthrough Chicago trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — A Malaysian doctor has led a breakthrough clinical trial in Chicago, where an experimental drug doubled one-year survival rates for patients with advanced pancreatic cancer, offering fresh hope against one of the deadliest cancers. Dr Devalingam Mahalingam, who heads the study at Northwestern University, said the treatment significantly improved outcomes among patients with advanced pancreatic cancer, a disease where most survive less than a year after diagnosis. “Pancreatic cancer remains one of the most challenging solid tumours to treat, but these findings provide cautious optimism,” Mahalingam said.

items.title: ‘I don’t remember’ — Inspector who ran over traffic cops claims bewitched by ‘black magic’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — A 40-year-old police inspector claimed memory loss due to “black magic” when he was charged today with the attempted murder of a traffic policeman and seven counts of reckless driving. Muhammad Kamal Raj Shanmugam Abdullah, who reportedly suffers from schizophrenia, saw no plea recorded at the Sessions and Magistrate’s Courts after allegedly ramming an officer with his car on April 10, Berita Harian reported. Due to his mental health history, the court has ordered a formal psychiatric evaluation to determine his fitness to stand trial.

items.title: Bomba Malaysia aims to fill all special operations vacancies by 2027
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, April 16 — The Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) aims to fill all vacancies within its special operations units by 2027 to bolster operational readiness and service delivery, said Deputy Director-General (Operations) Datuk Ahmad Izram Osman. The department is committed to closing staffing gaps across several critical units, including Hazardous Materials (HAZMAT), Chemical, Biological, Radiological and Nuclear (CBRNe), Water Rescue (PPDA), Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS), and K9 detection teams. “From now until 2027, the department is committed to fully staffing its special teams to ensure optimal readiness and strengthen their specialised functions,” Ahmad Izram said today.

items.title: Former bus driver, girlfriend remanded four days after viral PLUS video shows woman on driver’s lap
items.summary: JASIN, April 16 — A former express bus driver and a woman have been remanded for four days, from today until Sunday, to assist in investigations into alleged dangerous driving. The remand order was issued by Magistrate Mazana Sinin at the Jasin Magistrate’s Court to facilitate investigations under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987. The incident is believed to have taken place on April 12 at about 7.30 pm near the southbound entrance of the Bemban Rest and Service (R&R) area.

items.title: Penang sees RM23.8m in fire losses for Q1 2026
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, April 16 — Penang recorded an estimated RM23.86 million in losses due to fires in the first three months of 2026, according to the Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba). Fire and Rescue Department Deputy Director-General (Operations) Datuk Ahmad Izram Osman revealed that the state received 2,434 calls between January and March, comprising 1,360 fire cases, 927 rescue cases, and 136 special service operations. Speaking to reporters after officiating the Penang Fire and Rescue Department Excellent Service Award Ceremony 2025 at PICCA @ Butterworth Arena today, Ahmad Izram highlighted a persistent issue: hoax calls.

items.title: Perak MB: No new forest plantation areas despite 100,000ha federal approval in 2012
items.summary: IPOH, April 16 — The Perak government has no plans to expand or open new areas for the Forest Plantation Development Zone (ZPLH) within the state’s Permanent Forest Reserve (PFR), the State Legislative Assembly was told today. Of the 31,566 hectares already approved for development, 8,810 hectares have been utilised, with 8,184 hectares fully planted and another 626 hectares currently in the process of planting. Saarani identified three main timber species that dominate the state’s current plantations: Getah Klon Balak (Hevea spp.

items.title: Inspector charged with attempted murder of traffic cop, sent for psychiatric evaluation
items.summary: Sessions Court Judge Mazuliana Abdul Rashid issued the order today after lawyer G. Nanda Goban informed the court that his client, 40-year-old Kamal Raj Abdullah, was diagnosed with schizophrenia three years ago, Free Malaysia Today reported. Kamal was charged earlier today with the attempted murder of traffic policeman Roslan Salleh, 27. The court has fixed May 14 for mention.

items.title: Cheap but out of reach: Why Malaysians can’t quit their cars despite affordable public transport
items.summary: The Ipsos Mobility Monitor 2026, based on a representative sample of 500 Malaysian adults, found that 77 per cent of Malaysians rely on private vehicles as their primary mode of transport, which dwarfs the global average of 54 per cent. Approximately 62 per cent of Malaysians agree that public transport in their area is affordable, significantly higher than the global sentiment of 53 per cent. More than half of Malaysians (55 per cent) reported that limited transport options make it difficult to reach their destinations, compared to 47 per cent of commuters globally.

items.title: Anwar hosts Australian PM Albanese for energy security talks amid US-Iran tensions
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 16 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today held a four-eyed meeting with his Australian counterpart Anthony Albanese, centred on strengthening bilateral ties and navigating emerging energy security challenges amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in West Asia. Australia remains a key economic partner for Malaysia, ranking as its 12th largest trading partner in 2025, with total bilateral trade valued at RM78.63 billion (US$18.38 billion). Of this, Malaysia's exports stood at RM48.64 billion (US$11.37 billion), while imports totalled RM29.99 billion (US$7 billion).

items.title: Too hot in here: Universiti Malaya apologises, disqualifies theatre performance over steamy bed scene
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 16 — Universiti Malaya (UM) has issued a formal apology following a public outcry over a suggestive theatre scene that went viral on social media, clarifying that the performance had deviated from its original, vetted script. The controversy stems from a clip of a performance during the Universiti Malaya Theatre Carnival (KARVITER). The footage showed a male and female actor alone together on a bed under dimmed lighting.

items.title: Red bays are public after 6.30pm — MPAJ warns businesses against ‘chop’ culture following Ampang Point ops
items.summary: AMPANG JAYA, April 16 — Business owners in Ampang Jaya are reminded that special parking bays marked in red are only for their exclusive use during specific daytime hours, and any attempt to block these spots at night or on weekends is an offence. According to the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council (MPAJ), red-marked special bays are strictly enforced for traders only from Monday to Friday, between 8.00 am and 6.30 pm. While red bays offer flexibility for public use after hours, MPAJ clarified that special bays marked in orange are subject to different rules.

items.title: How much was your raise? Malaysia’s 50 richest see wealth rocket up 30pc to US$116b
items.summary: According to the 2026 Forbes list of Malaysia’s 50 Richest, the total net worth of the group rose from US$90 billion last year, underpinned by a 5.2 per cent GDP expansion in 2025 and a strengthening ringgit. His Kuok Group, which spans agribusiness, hospitality, and data centres, saw its valuation rise by 19 per cent to US$13.6 billion. items.title: Penang CM: Physical works for next phases of Juru-Sungai Dua decongestion project to kick off in Q3
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 16 — Physical works for the next four packages of the Juru–Sungai Dua Toll Plaza Traffic Dispersal Project (PTJSD) are expected to commence in the third quarter of this year, according to Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. The expansion of the project, covering Package 2 through to Package 5, will complete the critical alignment from the Juru Toll Plaza to Sungai Dua. Providing an update on the project’s progress, the chief minister noted that Package 1 is already underway.

items.title: 26 drums of chemical waste found in Felcra Air Hitam palm plantation in Negeri Sembilan, DOE investigates dumping
items.summary: JEMPOL, April 16 — A total of 26 drums containing chemical waste were discovered in an oil palm plantation in Felcra Air Hitam, Seri Jempol, here yesterday. “Initial inspection finds that the drums contain unidentified chemicals, prompting assistance from the Hazardous Materials (Hazmat) special team from the Seremban 2 station. Meanwhile, Negeri Sembilan DOE director Abdul Aziz Parmin said all the drums remained at the location under the supervision of the authorities.
